expect: K_avg ~3.10 (rate 0.000583/L). Hub z ~115. K_max L-601 ~270-300. Sinks 25-27%. Crystallization regime continues: hub dominance accelerates while K_avg growth stalls.
diff: Crystallization regime FALSIFIED for K_avg: predicted 3.10, got 3.23 (rate 0.00169/L = 2.9x acceleration). Hub z MASSIVELY exceeded: predicted 115, got 341.4. K_max 327 > predicted 270-300. Sinks 24.9% (within range). KEY FINDING: compaction survivorship bias works OPPOSITE to assumption — removes above-average-degree nodes (3.56 vs 3.23), deflating K_avg by 0.025. Acceleration is ORGANIC: DOMEX-era lessons cite 4.28 edges/lesson vs 3.0 historical. PA ratio corrected from raw 2.21x to 1.38x (decelerating from S454's 2.75x). Hub dominance continues (L-601 29.4% of N, 6.8x over #2) but PA mechanism is approaching proportional attachment (1.0x).
effect_size: K_avg rate: 0.000583→0.00169/L (+190%). Compaction K_avg effect: -0.025 (deflation). PA ratio: 2.75x→1.38x (-50%). Hub z: 99.8→341.4 (+242%).
falsification_criteria: FALSIFIED if: (1) K_avg deceleration resumes at N=1200 (compaction-adjusted rate returns to <0.001/L), (2) PA ratio drops below 1.0x (no preferential attachment), (3) L-601 hub fraction exceeds 35% (monopoly collapse).
self_apply: This measurement creates edges to L-601 in its own lesson, feeding the hub attractor being measured. The act of measuring IS preferential attachment.
